Renoticed Mtn 1)For Relief From Default; 2)For Relief From Default Judgment; And 3)For Dismissal Of Actions Or In The Alternative For Stay Of Action
Off Calendar Aug-15-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEAR: Stephen C. Becker, Esq. 434-8000 for pltf & opposing party; James C. Parker, Esq. 248-0360 for deft & moving party.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Order signed in open court. (Pltf to submit further order to be reviewed by other counsel re factual findings.) (AJR/KH)

Renoticed Mtn 1)For Relief From Default; 2)For Relief From Default Judgment; And 3)For Dismissal Of Actions Or In The Alternative For Stay Of Action
Off Calendar Jul-26-2002 Continued To Aug-15-02 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James C. Parker, Esq., BRYDON LAW GROUP, 415-248-0360, appeared on behalf of defendant and moving party William M. Buchanan, Jr. Stephen C. Becker, Esq. BECKER LAW OFFICE, 415-434-8000, appeared on behalf of plaintiff and opposing party Jackan, L.C.

Based upon the agreement of parties a stay was granted. This matter is continued for further consideration of defendant's motion to set aside default. Supplemental papers shall be filed by August 2, 2002. Any reply papers shall be filed by August 8, 2002. William Buchanan's Motion to set aside default will be heard on August 15, 2002 in Department 502. (AJR/KH) Reporter: Patricia Evezich CSR# 1193
